HUTTON, WILLIAM S. M.—Appointed clerk in the customs' department, South Australia, 1850; inspector of police, 1855; clerk in registrar-general's department, 1856; secretary to the treasurer, 1857; secretary to the commissioner of public works, 1860.
HYDE, CHARLES TUNSTALL.—Was private secretary and aide-de-camp to Governor Hill at Antigua; captain, 6th West York militia.
ILES, J. A.—Colonial secretary, and member of executive council of the island of Nevis.
IMLACH, ROBERT WIGHT.—Crown solicitor, British Guiana.
INGLIS, JOHN.—Engineer foreman to the Mint in Hong Kong, 1864.
INGLIS, WILLIAM R.—Was appointed a stipendiary magistrate at the Bahamas, 1850; and president of the Turks Islands, Jan. 1854; and receiver-general, British Guiana, 1862.

INGLOTT, FERDINANDO V.—Was appointed a teacher of a government primary school at Malta, Nov. 1841; served in the naval department from 1843 to 1855; assistant superintendent of ports, 1858; and appointed comptroller of charitable institutions, Jan. 1859. Is ex-officio member of council.

INGRAM, THOS. LEWIS.—Served as acting clerk of customs; from 28th Aug. 1854 until Sept. 1855; queen's advocate at the Gambia, May. 1863; is a member of the Honourable Society of the Middle Temple; appointed tide surveyor, superintendent of quarantine, and clerk of the courts, on the 10th Feb. 1857; 1st lieutenant R. G. M. A., 9th Dec. 1859; captain of ditto, 22nd July, 1860; served under Colonel D'Arcy during the Badiboo war, in command of the rocket guns on the left, with the skirmishers in advance, at the taking of Kenty-Cunda and Sabba, and assisted at the
storming of the latter town, on the 21st Feb. 1861; author of a work on Light Field Gun Drill.
INNES, F. MAITLAND.—Colonial treasurer of Tasmania, appointed April, 1857.

INNES, J. R.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of Uitenhage division, Cape of Good Hope; appointed Nov. 1863; also inspector of roads; was teacher in government schools, 1842; clerk to superintendent general of education, 1845; clerk in colonial secretary's office, 1847 to 1856; magistrate of Riversdale, 1856-1863.

IRVING, CHARLES J.—Was in the colonial land and emigration office from June 1852 to Nov. 1853; clerk in the audit office, Somerset House, from Nov. 1853 to July 1864, when he was appointed special clerk, audit office, Mauritius.
IRVING, EDWARD.—Appointed private secretary to Mr. F. Elliot, assistant under-secretary of state for the colonies, 1854; appointed to establishment of the office, 1860; 1st junr. class, 1 Oct. 1866.

IRVING, HENRY T.—Appointed clerk in colonial office, Nov. 1854; promoted to 3rd class, Oct. 1859; appointed private secretary to Sir F. Rogers, under secretary of state, 1862; promoted to second class, April, 1863; appointed colonial secretary of Jamaica, June, 1866.
ISKENIUS, W. J.—1st clerk in customs' department, British Guiana; appointed 1856. First appointment in customs' department, 1854.

ISNARD, L.—Clerk in registrar's department, Mauritius, May, 1854; clerk to the second puisne judge of supreme court of that island, April, 1859; clerk to first puisne judge, 1863; appointed 1st clerk to the registrar, 1st March, 1864.

JACOBS, SIMEON.—Obtained a licence from the Hon. Society of the Inner Temple to practise as a special pleader, Nov. 1851; called to the bar, 17th Nov. 1852; admitted as an advocate of the supreme court, Cape of Good Hope, Dec. 1860; appointed attorney-general of British Kaffraria, 4th April, 1861.
JACQUES, EDWIN.—Appointed surgeon superintendent of quarantine, Sept. 1858; police surgeon at Port Louis, Mauritius, 1861.
JADIS, VANE.—Appointed clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, Aug. 1827; promoted assistant clerk, April, 1846.

JAMAICA, 2nd BISHOP OF. Founded 1824.—RIGHT REV. AUBREY GEORGE SPENCER, D.D.—Educated at Magdalen Hall, Oxford; was Bishop of Newfoundland from 1839 to 1843, when he was translated to this see; appointed a privy councillor in Jamaica, 1855. The diocese includes Jamaica and the colony of British Honduras.

JANISCH, H. R.—Police magistrate and clerk in colonial secretary's office, St. Helena; date of appointments, 1851 and 1845; was acting Queen's advocate 1856, and acting colonial secretary, 1861; has been in colonial service since 1838.

JAYETILLEKE, F.—Was appointed acting commissioner of requests, and police magistrate at Calpentyn, Ceylon, Jan. 1861, and confirmed in the same year; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate at Ballepittymodere, 1862; confirmed June, 1864; district judge, Chilan, 1865.
JEAN, THOS. V.—Assistant colonial secretary of Tasmania, appointed 1857.

JEFFREY, W. J.—Was appointed commissary of taxation in British Guiana, 1841; acting stipendiary magistrate, 1844; commissary of taxation for the county of Essequibo, 1846; commissary of taxation for Berbice, 1855; acting resident commissioner of the penal establishment, 1856; registrar of population and property for Berbice, 1857;
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
and assistant receiver-general of Berbice, 1859; stipendiary magistrate, 1864.

JENNER, GEORGE.—Was for 10 years, from 1848 to 1858, in H.M.'s 4th regt.; was attached to the land transport corps during the Crimean war; appointed inspector of police at the Mauritius, Feb. 1858; and police magistrate at Rodrigues, June, 1862.
JENNINGS, J. H.—Chief clerk, government office, St. Lucia, 1861; formerly stipendiary magistrate, St. Lucia; appointed 1849. Was first appointed clerk in government office, St. Lucia, 1837.

JENNINGS, MATTHEW.—Appointed clerk in the customs' department, Cape Town, Sept. 1848; acting sub-collector at Port Beaufort, 1850; sub-collector, East London, 1851; controller of customs and navigation laws, 1852; shipping-master and justice of the peace for the port of East London, 1855; resident magistrate of the district of East London, 1857; immigration agent, 1858; collector of customs for British Kaffraria, 1859.
JEWELL, R. R.—Clerk of the works in Western Australia; appointed 1853.
JOACHIM, FRANCIS.—Entered the police force in Mauritius, December, 1841, appointed to immigration department, April 1847; promoted to be dépôt accountant, July, 1854.

JOHNSON, THOMAS.—Appointed clerk to the government steamer, Gambia, 1847; third writer in 1852; colonial registrar 1855; registrar of deeds by her majesty's warrant on 7th June, 1858; and lieutenant of the Combo militia, 12th April, 1862; Mr. Johnson has served as second writer in 1852; as clerk of the market in 1855, and as postmaster in 1859; second writer, and collector of rates, Dec. 1862.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER R.—Was in the civil service of the Mauritius from the beginning of 1828 until the end of 1833; he then proceeded with the late Lord Napier to China, being appointed his lordship's private secretary; and, in 1835, he became third commissioner, which office he held until appointed deputy superintendent of the trade of British subjects in China in May, 1837; in the latter capacity he was actively employed during the war in China (for which he received the medal), and in superintending the trade in the South during the absence of the chief superintendent in the North; he also administered the government of Hong Kong for upwards of a year, from the first acquisition of that island to its being finally taken over by the colonial department; since 1843, he filled the office of secretary and registrar to the superintendency of trade in China; and on the abolition of that office, Sept. 25, 1852, he obtained a compensation allowance.
JOHNSTON, H. W.—Assistant clerk in the provincial secretary's office, Nova Scotia.

JOHNSTON, JAMES.—Stipendiary magistrate, St. Lucia; appointed 1849. Was judge of district court of requests, judge of court of appeal, and judge of court of petty sessions; appointed clerk in government office, St. Lucia, 1818; retired on a pension, 1862.
JOHNSTON, J. W.—Attorney-general of Nova Scotia, and member of the executive council and house of assembly.
JOHNSTONE, W.—Police magistrate, northern district, Dominica.
JOLLIVET, ISIDORE.—District magistrate, Mauritius, February, 1862.
JONES, JOHN PEYTON.—Police magistrate at Westbury, Tasmania; appointed 1841.
JONES, R. S. W.—Brevet-major 2nd West India regiment; appointed acting assistant police magis-
trate, Sierra Leone, 1861; manager of the first eastern districts of that colony, assistant police magistrate, 1862.

JORDON, E., C.B. (creat., 18th May, 1860).—Was for several years a member of the executive committee, Jamaica, but resigned in 1860; reappointed in April 1863; resigned in Oct. 1864; appointed receiver general in Oct. 1864, and resigned in Aug. 1865, when he was appointed island secretary and governor's secretary.

JOSEPH, SIDNEY.—Appointed clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, July, 1843; was private secretary to Rt. Hon. Sir Jas. Stephen for seven years, and subsequently acted in the same capacity to Mr. Merivale for ten years; promoted assistant clerk, Nov. 1858; is also parliamentary clerk of the colonial office.
JULYAN, PENROSE G.—Crown agent for the colonies, appointed 1859; was for many years in the commissariat department at home and in Canada.
JUMEAUX, ARTHUR.—Writer, Ceylon civil service, 1865; and acting police magistrate, Pan-tura.

KEATE, ROBERT WM.—Educated at Eton and Christchurch, Oxford; was appointed civil commissioner at the Seychelles Islands, 1849; lieut.-governor of Grenada, 1853; and governor, commander-in-chief, and vice-admiral of Trinidad, from Nov. 1856 to April 1864. Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1844; governor of Natal, Nov., 1866.

KEENS, JAMES HENRY.—President of the privy council; assistant justice of the court of common pleas; member of the legislative council, 1845; and a member of the executive committee, Tobago; was master in chancery from 1837 to 1853; police magistrate from 1838 to 1856; administered the government of Tobago for ten months in 1856 and 1857.
KELLY, L. F.—Appointed deputy assistant commissary-general at Trincomalee, Ceylon, 1863; acting commissioner of request, and police magistrate, Chavagacherry, 1865.
KELLY, WM. FRED. THE REV., M.A.—Was colonial chaplain of Matelle, Ceylon; appointed by the bishop of Colombo to the chaplaincy of Nuwera Ellia, 1864.
KEMBLE, W.—Clerk of the house of assembly at Jamaica; appointed 1854. Was appointed sergeant-at-arms to the assembly, 1844.
KENDRICK, CAPT. J. H.—Superintendent of lighthouses, Nova Scotia.

KENNEDY, ARTHUR E. ESQ., C.B., 1862.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin. Entered the army as ensign in the 11th regiment in 1827; lieutenant 1832; captain 68th light infantry, 1840; retired from the army in 1848 to accept civil employment; was selected (while serving in the army), in 1846 to fill the office of county inspector, under the board of works; and served uninterruptedly during the Irish famine, as inspector under Sir John Burgoyne's relief commissioner; and subsequently as inspector of poor laws, till the office was abolished in 1851; appointed Governor of the "Gambia," and transferred to Sierra Leone in 1852; consul-general of the adjoining Sherbro Country, in 1854; and removed in the same year to the government of Western Australia; administered the latter government without interruption till February, 1862; created companion of the Bath in 1862; and appointed governor of Vancouver's Island, November, 1863.
KEON, MILES GERALD.—Appointed colonial secretary of Bermuda, March, 1859; and member
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.

of council in that colony, August, 1859; is corresponding member of the literary and scientific society of Abbeville in France; author of several educational and historical works; was for some years connected with the Daily Press and Quarterly Reviews in London, in their leading and literary departments.

KER, ALAN.—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple, Nov. 1842; chief justice of Nevis, 1854; appointed chief justice of Dominica, 1856; and assistant judge of the supreme court of Jamaica, 1861; acted as attorney-general of Antigua, from August, 1853, to March, 1854.

KER, ROBERT.—Entered the colonial service of British Columbia as clerk in the treasury, May, 1859; appointed to be chief clerk in the audit department of the colony, July, 1860; appointed acting auditor-general, June 1864; and auditor-general, July 1865.
KERR, CLAUDE.—Late captain 4th West India regiment; superintendent of Her Majesty's penal settlement, Demerara, 1863.

KERR, W. W. RALEIGH.—Was appointed assistant auditor-general at Mauritius, May, 1843; acting auditor-general, Jan. 1845; appointed auditor-general, Nov. 1846, and treasurer of that island, April, 1861.
KERR, THOMAS.—Police magistrate in the parish of Christ Church, Barbados.
KEYT, F.—Medical assistant, Ceylon, 1863.
KING, FRANCIS O.—First assistant—sworn clerk, Registrar's Office, Demerara.

KILLIKELLY, WM. EDW.—Educated for the army at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst. Was private secretary and aide-de-camp to his father during his administration of the government of St. Kitt's in 1833; entered the service of the imperial customs at Demerara in 1835; promoted in same department to St. Kitt's in 1836; where he continued to serve until 1844, when he was promoted to be comptroller and landing surveyor at Grenada; specially appointed to be acting collector at Grenada in 1845, and comptroller of navigation laws in 1849; appointed comptroller of colonial customs in 1855; was private secretary to Lieut.-governor Kortwright from June 1859 to Jan. 1864; holds the same appointment under Lt.-governor Mundy; is a justice of the peace; a member of assembly; aide-de-camp to the lieut.-governor; appointed police magistrate of the northern district, Grenada, Aug. 1865.

KINDER, THOS. WM.—Established the railway works at Bromsgrove and Oldbury in 1845; subsequently conducted locomotive department, and working of Shrewsbury and Birmingham railway; had the management of the locomotive department of the Midland Great Western Railway of Ireland, under contract, from 1851 to 1855; ensign Worcester militia, 1840; Lieutenant, 1846; Captain, 1853; on disembodiment of the Worcestershire regiment, was appointed captain in the third West York militia, 1859; elected associate of institution of civil engineers, 1860; appointed master of the Mint, Hong-Kong, Nov. 1863.
KING, Æ. A.—Appointed a writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1865, and in the same year acting police magistrate of Upper and Lower Doombera; acting assistant government agent, Kandy, 1866.

KING, J. HAMPDEN.—Graduated at Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1843; was a member of the house of assembly in Barbados from 1847 to 1853, and again in 1862; appointed a judge of the court of appeal at Barbados, 1858; acted as colonial secretary from April, 1860, to March, 1862; acted as auditor-general from March
to May, 1862; appointed to administer the government of St. Kitt's during the absence of Sir B. C. C. Pine, 6th Aug. 1862.
KINGS, H. R. V.—Assistant surveyor-general. —Appointed 1855; was in the department from 1838.

KINGSTON (JAMAICA), 1st BISHOP OF. Founded 1856.—RIGHT REV. REGINALD COURTENAY, D.D.—Educated at Magdalen Hall, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. 1835, M.A. 1838; appointed archdeacon of Middlesex in Jamaica in 1853; and coadjutor to the Bishop of Jamaica, under the title of Bishop of Kingston, Feb. 1856; a privy councilor in Jamaica, Sept. 1856.
KIRK, JAMES.—Senior member of the privy council of Tobago.

KIRKWALL, VISCOUNT, C. M. G.—Entered the army as an ensign in the 92nd Highlanders, Aug. 8, 1845; lieutenant, Nov. 9, 1846; captain, Nov. 1853; exchanged into the 71st Highland light infantry in 1854; served with that regiment in the trenches before Sebastopol; was at the taking of Kertch and Yanakale; received a medal and clasp for Sebastopol, and Turkish medal was granted March 2, 1858; Her Majesty's royal licence and permission to accept and wear the insignia of the 5th class of the imperial order of the Medjidie, which his Imperial Majesty the Sultan was pleased to confer upon him, as a mark of his Majesty's approbation of his distinguished services before the enemy in the late war. Served as aide-de-camp to Sir Henry Ward, G.C.M.G., then lord high commissioner in the Ionian Islands, from 1851 to 1854; appointed to the Scots fusilier guards in 1856; sold out of the army in 1857; is a deputy-lieutenant of Ayrshire, and a captain in the royal Bucks yeomanry cavalry.
KITTERY, A. S.—Clerk in the slave registration department, Mauritius, May, 1827; clerk in the customs, 1829, and cashier of the registration and mortgage department, 1845.
KNELLER, G. H.—Appointed collector of customs at the Gambia, May, 1858; is also superintendent of pilotage, and registrar supreme court.
KNIGHT, W.—Was appointed chief clerk in colonial secretary's office, Western Australia, June, 1831; assistant auditor-general, 1843; and auditor-general, Nov. 1846.
KNIGHT, WILLIAM.—Keeper of the gaol, Georgetown, British Guiana, was town agent to the penal settlement.

KNOWLYS, H. B.—Appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, Feb. 1859; commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Upper and Lower Doombera, 1862; acting at Harispattoo, 1864. Resigned appointment in 1866.
KNOX, W. G.—Called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1831; appointed solicitor-general of Trinidad, 1845; puisne judge, 1846; and chief justice of the colony, 1849.
KNYS, H. R. V.—Deputy surveyor-general, Cape of Good Hope; appointed November 1863; has been in that department since 1838.
KOCH, E. L.—Appointed medical assistant to Ceylon government, 1862.

KORTRIGHT, CORNELIUS H.—Appointed a local magistrate at the Bahamas, 18; president administering the government of the Virgin Islands, 1854; lieut.-governor of Grenada, Nov. 1856; appointed to be lieut.-governor of Tobago, April, 1864.
KRIEKENBEEK, C. A.—Lic. Med. & Surg., Bengal College; appointed medical sub-assistant to the government of Ceylon; 3rd class, 1843;
Digitized by Google
Q 2
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
2nd class, 1845; 1st class, 1854; assistant colonial surgeon, 1858.
KRIEKENBEEK, J.—Assistant auditor-general, Ceylon, April, 1852.
KYSHE, J. B.—Assistant district clerk, Mauritius, August, 1857; clerk, colonial secretary's office, August, 1859; financial clerk, December, 1860.
LA BLACHE, CHARLES.—Was principal officer of police at Seychelles, August, 1839; district clerk, Mauritius, November, 1856; appointed stipendiary magistrate, Feb. 1859.

LABORDE, EDWARD.—Colonial secretary, registrar of deeds, clerk of the courts, crown, and council of St. Vincent; appointed 1861. First entered the colonial service as clerk in the treasury department of that island, 1850; appointed deputy treasurer, 1852; acting colonial secretary in 1856; police magistrate, 1857; stipendiary magistrate and coroner, 1858; provost-marshal, 1860; is the captain commanding the St. George's volunteers, which were raised by him in 1863.

LABORDE, REV. H. W.—Rector of St. George's and St. Andrew's parishes, and chaplain of the gaol, St. Vincent, appointed 1852; M.A. Cambridge, where he graduated B.A., 1845; was ordained assistant curate in St. Vincent, 1845; was minister of All Saints' chapel and garrison, chaplain of the island of Trinidad, 1850 to 1852; is chaplain to the Bishop of Barbados; was appointed the rural dean of St. Vincent, 1864; and also a member of the legislative council.
LABROOY, REV., E. C.—Colonial chaplain, Jaffna, Ceylon, 1862.

LABUAN, 1st BISHOP OF. Consecrated 1855.—RIGHT REV. FRANCIS THOMAS MACDOUGALL, D.C.L. —Educated for the medical profession at King's College, London, where he received the gold medal for general medical proficiency in 1837; was appointed demonstrator of anatomy there, and elected an associate; was admitted a member of the college of surgeons, 1839, and elected a fellow in 1854; was for some time at Magdalen Hall, Oxford, whence he received the degree of M.A. 1845, and hon. D.C.L. 1854; after having for some time had the management of some mining concerns in South Wales, he entered the church in 1845, and was successively curate of Fromingham, Pigott, and Lakenham, Norwich, and Christchurch, Bloomsbury; chief missionary to Borneo, 1847; commissary of the Bishop of London in Borneo, 1853-4; and 1st Bishop of Labuan, 1855. The diocese consists of the island of Labuan and its dependencies.

LA CAZE, LOUIS.—Attorney-general of St. Lucia; appointed 1850. Is a member of the executive and legislative councils; first appointed solicitor-general in 1842; has acted as puisne judge in the absence on leave of the judge, and also as chief justice on the resignation of Sir R. B. Clarke.
LAIDE, ISAAC.—Revenue officer, Granada, Nov. 1858; acting auditor, May 1865.
LAIDLAW, HENRY.—Educated at the University of Edinburgh; senior stipendiary magistrate, Jamaica; appointed 1833. Also agent of immigration.
LAING, J.—Resident surgeon, Somerset hospital, Cape of Good Hope; has held minor medical appointments since 1827.
LAMOND, G.—Appointed clerk in the audit office, Natal, 1853; and second clerk in the office of colonial secretary of that colony, 1859; 1st clerk, 1864.
LAMOTHE, CHAS.—Appointed inspector of
distilleries at Mauritius, Jan. 1856; acting superintendent of same, Sept. 1862; superintendent, July, 1863.
LANGLOIS, H.—Clerk and draughtsman in surveyor-general's department at Mauritius; appointed 1840.
LANGTON, JOHN, M.A.—Auditor of public accounts, Canada; sometime vice-chancellor of the University of Toronto, Canada West.
LARDNER, W. G.—Appointed private secretary to Lieutenant-Governor Price, Dominica, 1862.
LATOUCHE, FREDERICK A.—Appointed clerk in harbour-master's department at Mauritius, Oct. 1839; promoted to be first clerk, July, 1851.
LAVERS, JAMES.—Appointed clerk in the customs at Mauritius, Oct., 1857; promoted, Feb. 1859; senior clerk, Sept. 1865.

LAWSON, GEORGE.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; graduated B.A. in mathematical honours in 1844; was called to the bar at the Middle Temple, 1847; was appointed acting deputy Queen's advocate for the southern circuit, Ceylon, May, 1849; acting deputy Queen's advocate, Kandy, Dec. 1849, and so continued till confirmed in May, 1852; acting district judge, Colombo, Jan. 1857; he acted as second puisne judge of the supreme court in Jan. 1860; and in June of the same year resumed his duties on the bench at Colombo; resumed duties as deputy Queen's advocate, Kandy, 1861; and appointed district judge of Colombo, Jan. 1, 1863.

LAWSON, T. G.—Appointed public native interpreter, Sierra Leone, May, 1852; police serjeant, 1854; 2nd inspector of police, 1854; chief inspector of police, 1856; resumed the duties of public native interpreter, with an increased salary, 1861.
LAYARD, C. H.—Deputy assistant commissary, Galle, Ceylon, Nov. 1863.

LAYARD, C. P.—Was formerly at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed extra-assistant in the colonial secretary's office in Ceylon, 1830; fiscal and magistrate at Jaffna, 1831; assistant to the collector of Colombo, 1832; assistant government agent and district judge, at Caltura, 1836; assistant government agent and district judge, Negombo, 1837; district judge, Galle, 1839; district judge of Trincomalee, 1840; district judge of Galle, 1850; government agent, western province, 1851.

LAYARD, F.—Appointed a writer to Ceylon government, Jan. 1846; commissioner of requests at Matura, 1847; acting assistant government agent, Kandy, 1849; acting district judge, Caltura, Jan. 1851; and assistant government agent, commissioner of requests, and police magistrate, in July of the same year; assistant government agent, Badulla, 1858; retired on pension, 1862.

LEACH, JOHN VINCENT.—Is a barrister of the Inner Temple; deputy provost marshal-general for Jamaica from 1839 to 1854; holding absolutely that office during the administration of Lord Elgin and Sir C. E. Grey; was secretary to the main road board, 1863 to 1865; clerk of courts and keeper of public records for the colony of B. Honduras, provisionally in 1864; confirmed Feb. 1865; registrar of land titles for the colony.
LEAKE, G. W.—Crown solicitor, Western Australia; appointed Feb. 1860. On several occasions previous to his appointment, filled the office of acting crown solicitor.
LEE, E.—Was appointed chief constable of the police force, D'Urban, Natal, 1852; landing waiter in the customs' department, 1854; 1st landing waiter, 1860; warehouse-keeper, 1863.
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
LEE, L. F.—Appointed a writer in the Ceylon civil service, June, 1864; acting police magistrate, Doombera, March, 1865; acting assistant government agent, Galle, Aug. 1865.
LEES, JOHN C.—Was appointed assistant justice of the general court at Bahamas, 1821; also receiver of dues of Greenwich Hospital, 1828; and chief justice of the colony, 1836; retired, 1865.

LEFEVRE, K.C.B. (Civil). (Creat. 1857.) SIR JOHN GEORGE SHAW LEFEVRE.—Educated at Eton and Trinity Coll. Camb., where he was senior wrangler, 1818; fellow of Trinity, 1819; graduated M.A. 1821; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1824; elected a bencher, 1855; appointed under-secretary of state for the colonies, 1833; poor-law commissioner, 1834; joint-assistant secretary to the board of trade, 1841; deputy-clerk of the parliaments, 1848; clerk of the parliaments 1856. Is an ecclesiastical commissioner, and vice-chancellor of the university of London. Was a civil service commissioner, but resigned 1862.
LEFROY, A. O'GRADY.—Appointed treasurer of Western Australia, 1856. Was private secretary to Governor Fitzgerald, 1849 to 1855.
LEFROY, H. M.—Superintendent of convicts in Western Australia; appointed 1859. Was deputy superintendent from 1853 to 1859.

LEGH, RICHARD CORNWALL.—Entered the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1841; appointed assistant secretary at Malta, June, 1851; and auditor-general, June, 1858; is ex-officio a member of council. Acted as chief secretary to the government from July 11 to Oct. 17, 1855, and June 24 to Oct. 6, 1857. Was sent on a special and confidential mission to Sicily with reference to government contracts during the Crimean war.
LEGOY, NEMOURS.—Clerk in the internal revenue department, Mauritius, 1846; chief clerk in dépôt stipendiary magistrate's office, Nov. 1859.
LEITH, JAMES.—Assistant justice at Tobago; appointed 1858; is member of the privy and legislative councils.

LE-MARCHANT, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1838); G.C.M.G. 1860; K.C.B. 1865.—SIR JOHN GASPARD LE-MARCHANT.—Entered the army in 1821; became a colonel in 1851, and held local rank as a major-general till fully promoted to that rank in March, 1858; retired from the command of the 85th foot in 1846 on proceeding to Newfoundland; was governor and commander-in-chief of Newfoundland from Feb. 1847 to June, 1852; lieut.-governor of Nova Scotia from June, 1852, to Dec. 1857, when he was appointed governor of Malta, and received local rank of lieut.-general there, 1859; is a brigadier-general in the service of Her Catholic Majesty; a knight of the 1st class and a knight commander of the orders of San Fernando and of Charles III. of Spain; received the honour of knighthood from the Queen, with permission to wear his foreign orders, which were conferred for services in Spain.

LE PLASTRIER, C. J.—Is provost-marshal and postmaster at Tobago; appointed postmaster, 1834, and provost-marshal, 1837; is also usher to the privy and legislative councils; and marshal of the vice-admiralty court, Tobago.

LESAGE, JAMES.—Was a clerk in the audit office at Mauritius, April, 1852; appointed financial clerk in the colonial secretary's office, Feb. 1859; and junior examiner in the audit office, January 1860.
LESAGE, NAPOLEON.—Was clerk in the colonial secretary's office, at Mauritius, May, 1845; clerk in the audit office, Nov. 1847; clerk in the
registration and mortgage office, Jan., 1858; and chief clerk of that department, March, 1860.
LETOURNEUR, ADOLPHE.—Clerk in the treasury, Mauritius, 1st July, 1862.

LEWES, JOHN S.—Was employed in the admiralty department, June, 1837; appointed clerk to the agent-general for emigration, Feb. 1839; and clerk to the colonial land and emigration commissioners, Jan. 1840.
LEWIS, ALBERT.—Police and stipendiary magistrate, and coroner, St. Vincent; appointed 1856. First appointment as coroner in 1849.

LEWIS, ALLAN.—Was clerk in the colonial secretary's office, St. Vincent, 1845; immigration agent, 1848 to 1857; deputy registrar of deeds, 1854; appointed police and stipendiary magistrate and coroner in that colony, 1860.

LIDDLE, WM. FRED.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of Bedford, Cape of Good Hope; appointed 1857. Receives a pension from commissariat department; was private secretary to the governor of the Cape, April, 1852 to 1855.

LIESCHING, C. F. H. L.—Commissioner of requests, &c., of Ballepittymodere, in the colony of Ceylon, April, 1859; acting assisting agent, Galle, 1862; assistant agent at Jaffra, June, 1864; appointed assistant agent at Galle, May 1865, and acting police magistrate, Gampolla.

LIESCHING, L. F.—Appointed sub-collector and landing waiter at Trincomalie, Ceylon, June, 1855; commissioner of requests, &c., Point Pedro, 1855; acting deputy-fiscal, western province, 1856; acting district judge, &c., Badulla, 1857; resumed duties at Point Pedro, 1859; acting commissioner of requests, and police magistrate, at Jaffna, 1861; assistant government agent, Jaffna, 1862; acting district judge, Matura, 1863; district judge, Tangalle, June, 1864; acted as assistant government agent, Matura, in addition to judicial duties from Nov. 1864 to May 1865, when he resumed his duties at Tangalle.
LIGHT, K.C.B. (Civil. Creat. 1848).—SIR HENRY LIGHT.—Late governor and commander-in-chief of British Guiana.
LINDSAY, J. MAINWARING.—Was acting colonial secretary at the Gold Coast from Oct. 1859 to July, 1860, when he was appointed assistant colonial secretary, which he held until 1863.
LISTER, ALFRED.—Appointed to a cadetship in Hong Kong civil service, July 1865.
LITTLE, PHILIP F.—Assistant judge, Newfoundland; appointed 1858.

LLOYD, J. HOWARD F.—Appointed stipendiary magistrate for Dominica, Dec. 1835; inspector of prisons, 1838; assistant justice of the court of common pleas, July, 1853; member of council, 1861; acting district judge at Barticaoa, 1866.
LOCKHART, J. F.—Appointed police magistrate, Dominica, 1862; and is member of the executive and legislative councils.
LOFTUS, G.—Medical sub-assistant, Ceylon, 1830; medical assistant, 1858.
LOGAN, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856.)—SIR WILLIAM EDMOND LOGAN.—Director of the geological survey in Canada.

LOMAX, THOMAS.—Appointed October 26, 1863; clerk in the lands and works department in the colony of British Columbia, on his discharge from the corps of the royal engineers, having filled that situation since April, 1859.

LONGDEN, JAMES R.—Was appointed government clerk at the Falkland Islands, 1844, and acting colonial secretary, 1845, the duties of which office he performed until confirmed in the appointment, June, 1857; appointed president of the
Digitized by
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
Virgin Islands, Oct. 1861; and lieutenant-governor of Dominica, Aug. 1865.

LOOS, J.—Lic. Med. & Surg., Bengal College; Appointed medical sub-assistant to the government of Ceylon; 3rd class, Jan. 1843; 2nd class, 1845; 1st class, 1853; assistant colonial surgeon, 1858; acting colonial surgeon, Galle, 1862.

LORD, RICHARD H.—Was appointed manager of the 2nd eastern district, Sierra Leone. May, 1858. Is a magistrate, coroner, and commissioner of the court of requests in the rural districts of the colony and of the recently annexed territory of British Quiah. Is an officer in the royal Sierra Leone militia, and was in command of a company of that force during the late war in Quiah.
LOVEKIN, The Rev. ALFRED PETER, M.A. Oxford.—Appointed col. chaplain, Nuwera Ellia, Ceylon, 1859; promoted to the chaplaincy of Galle, 1864.

LOW, HUGH.—Treasurer of Labuan; appointed 1853. Is a member of the legislative council, police magistrate, and judge of the court in that colony; entered the colonial service in 1848; administered the government of Labuan during the governor's absence in 1863.

LOWE, ALEX. C.—Private secretary to the president of the Turks Islands; appointed 1849; is also a member of the board of education; and honorary secretary and treasurer of the same; a justice of the peace; and captain and adjutant in the Turks Islands volunteer corps.

LOWENFELD, G. G.—Has been in the colonial service since 1829; major of Berbice militia, 22nd July 1835; justice of the peace, 28th June 1836; acting sheriff of Berbice, 26th Sept. 1839; was emigration agent at Berbice from 1841 to 1846; financial accountant from 1846 to 1850; and receiver-general of British Guiana, 1850. Retired on pension, 1862.
LUCAS, G. A.—Was a captain in the 73rd regiment; appointed resident magistrate, Lower Umcomas, Natal, 1859, and removed to the same office in the Klip River of that colony, 1860.
LYNAR, H.—First clerk in the attorney-general's office at the Cape of Good Hope; appointed 1839. Is also clerk of the peace for the Cape division of the colony.
LYNCH, WM.—Stipendiary magistrate, Dominica; appointed 1835; is a member of council.

LYTTELTON, 4th BARON (Great Britain, Creat. 1794); BARON WESTCOTE, 1776 (Ireland); BARR., 1618 (England).—GEORGE WILLIAM LYTTELTON.—Was educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; bracketed senior classic, and chancellor's medallist in 1838; was under-secretary of state for the colonies from Feb. to July. 1846; is lord-lieut. and custos rotulorum of Worcestershire, and high steward of Bewdley; unsuccessfully contested the election for high steward of the University of Cambridge in 1840; was appointed principal of Queen's College, Birmingham, in 1845.

LYTTON, 1st BART. (United Kingdom, Creat. 1838); Privy Councillor. 1858.—RIGHT HON. SIR EDWARD GEORGE EARLE LYTTON BULWER-LYTTON.—Educated at Trinity Hall, Cambridge, where he graduated B.A. 1826, M.A. 1835; was M.P. for St. Ives in 1831, and for Lincoln from 1832 to 1841; has sat for Herts since July, 1852; appointed a deputy-lieut. of Herts, 1851; received the hon. degree of D.C.L. from Oxford in 1853; elected rector of the University of Glasgow, Nov. 1856 and Nov. 1858; his literary works are well known and very numerous; was secretary of state for the colonies from May, 1858, to June, 1859.
LYVEDEN, 1st BARON. (Creat. 1859.) Privy

Councillor.—ROBERT VERNON.—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford, where he was 2nd class in classics, 1822; was a lord of the treasury from Nov. 1830 to Nov. 1834; was secretary to the board of control from 1835 to 1839; and under-secretary for the colonies from 1839 to 1841; secretary at war for a few weeks in Feb. 1852; president of the board of control from March, 1855, to Feb. 1858; sat for Tralee from 1829 to 1831, and for Northampton from 1831 to 1859.
MAARTENSZ, A. G.—Medical practitioner, 1860; medical assistant to government of Ceylon, 1861.
MACK, J. P.—Appointed medical sub-assistant to government of Ceylon, 1856; medical assistant, 1858.
M'CANN, JOHN HENRY.—Appointed an inspector of police at Mauritius, March, 1848; clerk to the district magistrate at Grand Port, April, 1862.

MACARTHUR, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856.)—SIR WILLIAM MACARTHUR.—Was an elective member of the legislative council of New South Wales from 1848 to 1855; resigned his seat to proceed as special commissioner from New South Wales to attend the universal exhibition at Paris; received the decoration of the legion of honour; is author of a treatise on the cultivation of vineyards and the art of making wine.
MACARTNEY, W.—Served in the Irish constabulary; was appointed superintendent of police, Colombo, Ceylon, 1848; chief superintendent of police, Ceylon, 1858. Retired 1865.

MACARTHY, W. J.—Private secretary to Sir C. MacCarthy, governor of Ceylon, 1863; was formerly in the secretary's office of her Majesty's customs, London. Appointed registrar-general of lands, Ceylon, Dec. 1864 (provisional).
M'CLINTOCK, WM. C. H.—Superintendent of rivers and creeks, Pomeroon, British Guiana.

M'CORMACK, JOHN.—Appointed police magistrate at Sierra Leone, 1855. Is registrar of insolvent debtors' court, and coroner for a district of the colony; was sheriff and provost-marshal, 1853; superintendent of lighthouses, 1852.
M'COY, WM. H.—Colonial secretary and clerk of the council, Dominica; appointed 1849. Is also registrar, clerk of the Crown, and clerk of enrolments.
M'CREA, WILLIAM H.—Appointed chief clerk in the customs' department of British Columbia, May, 1859.
M'CRYSTAL, P.—Clerk in the post-office, Natal, 1859; clerk to the resident magistrate, Pietermaritzburg, in that colony, 1861.
MACDONALD, R. J. SOMERLED.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, a clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, June, 1859. Promoted to the 1st junior class, May 2, 1864.

MAC-DONNELL, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1855); C.B. (Civil) 1852.—SIR RICHARD GRAVES MAC-DONNELL.—Entered Trinity College, 1830; gained several honours both in classics and science when an undergraduate; obtained a scholarship in 1833; graduated B.A. 1835, M.A. 1838, hon. LL.D. 1844; called to the bar in Ireland, 1838, and at Lincoln's Inn in England, 1840; was chief justice of the Gambia from 1843 to 1847; governor of that colony from 1847 to 1851; conducted several exploring expeditions up the Gambia, and in the interior of Africa between that river and the Senegal; appointed governor of St. Lucia, 1852, and transferred to St. Vincent same year; appointed captain-general and governor-in-chief of South Australia, 1855, which government he held until March, 1862; ap-
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
pointed governor of Nova Scotia, April 1864; appointed governor of Hong Kong, 14th Oct. 1865.
MAC EACHINE, R.W.—Colonial engineer and surveyor, Tobago, 1853; is a member of the executive council.
MACFARLANE, J.—Appointed resident magistrate, county of Weenen, Natal, 1855. Is captain commanding the Weenen Yeoman Cavalry.

MACKGLASHAN, JOHN CHAS.—Clerk of the privy council of Jamaica; is also chief clerk in the office of the financial secretary; was appointed clerk in the executive commission office, in June, 1856, by Sir Charles Darling, K.C.B.; was, in April, 1866, on the death of the secretary of the executive committee and clerk privy council, appointed by Sir H. K. Storks, G.C.B., G.C.M.G., provisionally to these offices, pending the change in the constitution of the island, under which it became a crown colony; held these appointments until the inauguration of the new form of government; was appointed to his present offices by Sir J. P. Grant, K.C.B., on 16th September, 1866; is ensign in the Jamaica militia and 1st lieutenant in the volunteer force of the island.
M'GILL, E. F.—Appointed clerk in the surveyor-general's office, Natal, 1845, and clerk and draughtsman in the civil engineers' department of that colony, 1860.
M'INTOSH, J. J.—Colonial secretary and clerk of the council, Turks Islands; appointed 1849. Is also clerk of the crown and registrar of records; was police magistrate, 1844.
McINTYRE, J., M.D.—Medical officer, parish of St. Patrick, appointed 1858.
MCKINNEY, N. J.—Postmaster, Honduras, appointed 1859; is also secretary to the board of education; was acting inspector of police, 1860.
McMAHON, G. Y.—Junior professor, royal college, Mauritius May, 1864.
McPHERSON—Entered the police force, Mauritius, November 1841; police officer, May, 1854.
MACK, J. P.—Medical sub-assistant Ceylon, 1856; medical assistant, 1858.

MACKENZIE, JAMES GEORGE.—Is a captain of the Royal Navy on half-pay. Served as midshipman in H.M.S. sloop, 'Philomel,' at the battle of Navarino, and was frequently engaged in her boats against pirates in the Grecian Archipelago, for which services he was promoted to the rank of lieutenant in 1830, on passing his examination. Was flag-lieutenant to Admiral Sir Thomas Briggs, G.C.M.G. at Malta from 1834 to 1838; first lieutenant of H.M.S. 'Indus,' under captain Sir James Stirling, Knt., in the Mediterranean from 1840 to 1844; was commander of H.M.S. 'Caledonia,' in the squadron of evolution under Admiral Sir Charles Napier, 1847; in command of the Cornwall and Somerset coast-guard districts, 1848 to 1853; subsequently served in H.M.S. 'Princess Royal,' attached to the Baltic Fleet, in the war with Russia; appointed Governor of the Falkland Islands, Sept. 1862; Lieut.-Gov. St. Kitts, Nov. 1866.
MACLEAN, ARCHIBALD.—Judge of the Court of Queen's Bench, Upper Canada, Feb. 1856.

MACLEAN, JOHN, C.B. (Civil.)—Was appointed agent at Fort Peddie, Cape of Good Hope, Nov. 1845; commissioner for the T'Sambie tribe of Kaffirs, Jan. 1847; chief commissioner for British Kaffraria, Sept. 1852, and appointed lieut.-governor of those territories upon their erection into a separate colony, Dec. 1860; appointed lieut.-governor of Natal, April 6, 1864.
MACLEAR, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1860.)—SIR THOS.—Astronomer-royal at the Cape; knighted by patent.

M'LEOD, GEO. M.—Chief clerk in government office, St. Lucia; appointed 1857. Has been assistant clerk in same office since 1844; is secretary to the board of education; is stipendiary magistrate, and immigration agent, 1861; is presiding justice of the court of requests, inspector of prisons, coroner, &c., &c.
MACLEOD, JOHN.—Stipendiary magistrate, British Guiana; appointed 1834. Retired.

M'MILLAN, G. DOUGLAS.—Appointed stipendiary clerk, Mauritius, March, 1848; district clerk Seychelles, June, 1853; clerk to the district magistrate at Grand Port, Mauritius, June, 1856; clerk to the Flacq district, April, 1862.
McNAB, J. B. D.—Clerk to the chairman of the board of works, Nova Scotia.
MACNAB, JAMES.—Receiver-general of Nova Scotia, and member of executive council.
M'NULTY, WM., LL.D.—Police magistrate of George Town, British Guiana; appointed 1840. Retired.
MACPHAIL, W. B. U.—Collector of import revenue, Nevis; and member of the legislative council.

MACREADY, W. C.—Educated at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, Nov. 1854; extra assistant government agent and deputy fiscal, Kandy, 1855; acting assistant government agent Pultam, 1856; assistant agent at Kurnegalle, July 1858; acting assistant government agent, Colombo 1859; acting assistant government agent, Matura 1860; assistant government agent at Colombo Nov. 1860; district judge, &c., Chilaw, May, 1862 acting assistant agent and acting district judge, Putlam, 1863; resumed his duties at Chilaw, Nov. 1863; acting assistant government agent, Matura, 1st May, appointment confirmed, Dec., 1865; and appointed acting assistant government agent, &c., Putlam.

M'SWINEY, JOHN.—Sheriff of Berbice and stipendiary magistrate of the New Amsterdam judicial district, in British Guiana. Appointed at the close of 1835, by Lord Glenelg, government agent for establishing normal schools in the West Indies for the training of native teachers, and proceeded to the Bahamas; accepted this appointment on the same footing as a stipendiary magistrate, under the order in council as to emoluments; services transferred in 1838 to the trustees of the Mico charity on the same terms and was their agent, as well as the general superintendent of their schools in the Bahamas until 1839—in British Guiana until 1841—in Jamaica until 1842—and in the Windward Islands until 1843. The Parliamentary grant for education in the West Indies having been discontinued, he was required to discharge all the teachers and close all the country schools in Jamaica, most of which, however, he succeeded in transferring to other societies interested in the promotion of negro education, for the performance of which duty he received the approval of the trustees of the charity. In 1843 he performed a similar duty in Trinidad. From 1843 to 1848 was proprietor and editor of the 'Trinidad Standard' newspaper; was senior member of the town council of the port of Spain in 1848; appointed that year by Lord Grey inspector of schools in British Guiana, and in 1849 a stipendiary magistrate of British Guiana; was a commissioner of education in 1850; and in 1863 was appointed sheriff of Berbice.
MAGUIRE, THOMAS.—Appointed stipendiary magistrate at Mauritius, Jan. 1846, and police magistrate, May, 1861.
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
MAINWARING, A.—Writer in the Ceylon civil service Dec. 1865; and acting commandant of regiment, and police magistrate, Harrispatloo; joint police magistrate, Kornegalle, 1866.
MAKEHAM, JOHN M.—Was clerk to the South Australian colonization commission, Mar. 1840; appointed a clerk in colonial land and emigration commissioners' office, April, 1843.
MAKEIG, GEORGE ANDREWS.—Clerk, first class, post office, Tasmania, appointed May 21, 1840.
MANGEOT, E. A.—Appointed officer of police in Mauritius. August, 1839; civil commissary, January, 1848; district clerk at Black River, January, 1852.
MANGET, E. A.—Surgeon-general of British Guiana; appointed 1857, and allowed private practice. Was acting surgeon-general during 1853.

MANIKION, J. M.—Was a volunteer clerk in the council office at Mauritius, Jan. 1850; appointed a clerk in colonial secretary's office, Feb. 1853, and 2nd clerk in the council office in that island, Feb. 1856.
MANLEY, EDWARD JOHNSTONE.—Colonial auditor, Tasmania, appointed Aug. 20, 1853.
MANN, A. G. C.—Medical officer, northern district, Dominica.

MANN, GOTHIER KERR.—Engineer-in-chief, and superintendent, Fitzroy Dock establishment, Cockatoo Island; formerly of the Bombay regiment of artillery; entered the colonial service of New South Wales in 1847.
MANN, ROBERT JAMES, M.D., F.R.A.S.—Superintendent-general of education in Natal; appointed July, 1859.
MANN, W. AITON.—Appointed clerk of works at Mauritius, Sept. 1856.

MANNING, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1858.)—SIR WILLIAM MONTAGU MANNING, L.L.D.—Educated at Tavistock, at Southampton, and at University College, London; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1832; was chairman of quarter sessions in New South Wales from Oct. 1837 to Oct. 1844; solicitor-general there from Oct. 1844 to Jan. 1848; acting judge of the supreme court there from Jan. 1848 to Nov. 1849; again solicitor-general there from Nov. 1849 to May, 1856; attorney-general from May, 1856, to May, 1858; is a member of the executive council of New South Wales, and one of the Queen's counsel there; author of "Notes of Proceedings in Electoral Revision Courts," "Nevile and Manning's Reports in Court of King's Bench," 1832 to 1837.
MANNING, F. A.—Appointed superintendent of the melting department in the Hong Kong mint.

MANTELL, J. J.—Appointed chief justice at the Gambia settlement, Oct. 1847; is a barrister-at-law; was Queen's advocate, 1841 to 1847; on several occasions performed the duties of colonial secretary, and in 1844 administered the government of the settlement.
MAPLETON, H.—Was judge of the summary and police court, St. Helena; appointed 1847. Was formerly in the royal navy.

MARA, RICHARD WESTON, L.L.D.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; gained honours in science and classics; graduated B.A. in 1836, M.A. in 1839; LLD. in 1863; called to the Irish bar in 1840; appointed counsel to the attorney-general of Ireland in 1845; appointed attorney-general and judge advocate of Antigua in 1859; advocate-general and Queen's proctor, 1862; member of the executive council, and member of the house of assembly; is also Queen's counsel
of Montserrat; acted as chief justice of Antigua in the absence of Sir W. Snagg in 1863-4.

MARC, ALBERT CHARLES.—Was appointed a clerk in the post-office at Mauritius, Aug. 1851; clerk in the colonial secretary's office, May, 1855, and landing-waiter in the customs' department of that island, April, 1856.
MARCH, W. T.—Clerk of the supreme court and crown, Jamaica; appointed 1857.

MARGENOUT, J. W.—Licentiate of medical and surgical college, Bengal; appointed medical sub-assistant to governor of Ceylon, third class, 1850; second class, 1855; medical assistant, 1858; acting assistant colonial surgeon, 1862; appointed assistant colonial surgeon, 1865.
MARIN, HENRY.—Was clerk in the customs' department at Mauritius, June, 1854, and landing-waiter, Jan. 1858.
MARLET, EVENOR.—Appointed inspector of licences at Mauritius, Jan. 1840.
MARQUARD, L.—Examiner of diagrams, surveyor-general's department, Mauritius; appointed 1857

MARRIOTT, HUNT.—Emigration agent for British Guiana in Calcutta; appointed 1858; was previously an officer in the St. Helena artillery, then in the imperial customs, and has held various acting appointments in the service of British Guiana.
MARSH, J. H.—Appointed 2nd master of Queen's College and Colombo Academy, Ceylon, May, 1857; exchanged duties, temporarily, with the inspector of schools, 1860; resumed own duty 1862.

MARSH, W. H.—Clerk to procureur-general, Mauritius, Feb. 1848; crown solicitor, April, 1854; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, Oct. 1859; acting assistant colonial secretary, Sept. 1863; special immigration commissioner to India, May, 1865; junior assistant colonial secretary, April, 1866.

MARSHAL, HENRY WORGAN, M.A.—Principal of the grammar school and inspector of schools in the island of St. Kitts; educated at King's College, London, and Christ's College, Cambridge; B.A., 1855; M.A., 1858.
MARSHALL, THOMAS, C.—Superintendent of public works, Barbadoes; appointed 1860; and superintendent of fire brigade, 1862.
MARSTON, THOMAS.—Appointed a land commissioner, Sierra Leone, 1859; and master of the courts, 1860. Is registrar of the vice-admiralty court.
MARTIN, HENRY.—Harbour master, Grenada; appointed 1860.
MARTIN, JOHN SOMERS.—Coroner of Antigua; appointed 1848.
MARTIN, LOUIS.—Was volunteer in surveyor-general's department at Mauritius, Dec. 1859; appointed clerk in the audit office, Jan. 1860; junior examiner, March, 1862.
MARTINDALE, EDWARD H.—Was clerk to the stipendiary court, Mauritius, July, 1848; district clerk, Moka, July, 1854; appointed stipendiary magistrate, Plaines Wilhems, Sept. 1858.

MARTINDALE, FELIX DE R.—Was clerk in the Mauritius currency bank, June, 1848; clerk to marine court, Feb. 1849; clerk to stipendiary magistrate, Plaines Wilhems, March, 1850; district clerk, Moka, May, 1862.
MASON, Rev. W. L.—Civil chaplain, Mauritius, November, 1854; acting superintendent of Government schools, November, 1855; resumed duties as civil chaplain, May, 1857.
MASON, A. B.—Writer, Ceylon civil service,
Digitized by
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
1866; acting deputy fiscal, Colombo, in the same year.
MASSIE, ROBERT.—Appointed a writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1865; acting assistant government agent, Trincomalie, Jan. 1866; acting assistant government agent, Korneyselle, April, 1866.
MASSON, N. R.—Deputy registrar of the supreme court of Hong Kong; appointed 1856. Clerk in registrar-general's office, 1852.

MATHEW, GEORGE BENVENUTO BYAM.—Was appointed an ensign in the 52nd light infantry, July 7, 1825; was promoted to be lieutenant, unattached, Sept. 19, 1826; was appointed to the rifle brigade, Sept. 20, 1826; exchanged into the 70th foot, Nov. 26, 1828; was promoted to be captain, April 19, 1831; was appointed a lieutenant and captain in the Coldstream guards, July 26, 1833; captain in the 85th foot, June 17, 1836; captain, unattached, Sept. 23, 1836; and retired from the army April 9, 1841, as lieutenant and captain of the grenadier guards; he served in America and the Mediterranean; was returned M.P. for Athlone in 1835; and represented Shaftesbury from 1837 till 1841; was appointed governor and commander-in-chief in and over the Bahama Islands, March 26, 1844; consul at Charleston, in the United States of America, March 8, 1850; was consul at Philadelphia, from July 5, 1853, till May 28, 1856; when his exequatur was revoked by the president of the United States; was appointed consul-general for the Russian ports in the Black Sea, Oct. 31, 1856; and consul-general for the Russian ports in the Black Sea and sea of Azoff, to reside at Odessa, Sept. 14, 1857; and secretary to the legation in Mexico, July 24, 1858; was chargé d'affaires from Sept. 20, 1859, till May 25, 1861, when he was appointed chargé d'affaires and consul-general to the republics of Guatemala, Nicaragua, Costa Rica, Honduras, and Salvador; was promoted to be minister plenipotentiary to the republics in Central America, Aug. 21, 1861.

MAUNSELL, D. C.—Private secretary to Governor Seymour, British Columbia, appointed June 1864; private secretary to the Governor of Vancouver's Island, Jan. 1864; entered the Home Civil Service, Nov. 1853; in the office of the inspector-general of imports and exports; served under Her Majesty's Commissioner for the Exhibition of 1862 as assistant for finance and statistics.

MAURITIUS, BISHOP OF.—RIGHT REV. VINCENT WILLIAM RYAN, D.D.—Educated at Magdalen Hall, Oxford; where he was 2nd class in classics, 1840; and graduated M.A. 1848; held preferment for some years in the Channel Islands; subsequently head of the Liverpool institution; became principal of Highbury College, the metropolitan training institution; and was consecrated 1st Bishop of this see, 1854.

MAXSE, LIEUT.-COLONEL.—Appointed lieut.-governor of Heligoland in 1863; entered the army in 1849, and served in the Eastern campaign of 1854 as aide-de-camp to Lord Cardigan, including the battles of Alma and Balaclava (wounded), siege of Sebastopol; medal and clasps, 5th class of the Medjidié and Turkish medals.
MAXWELL, W. J.—Was appointed extra clerk in the customs' department, Sierra Leone, Oct. 1856; 2nd landing-waiter, 1859; acted as 2nd clerk and locker, May, 1860, to May, 1861.
MAY, CHARLES.—Superintendent of police, Hong Kong; appointed 1844; promoted first police magistrate, 1862; and appointed sheriff of Hong Kong, Jan. 1864.
MAYER, G. C.—Acting district magistrate, Seychelles, Jan. 1865; district magistrate, Mauritius, Oct. 1865.

MEADE, JAMES.—Colonial secretary and treasurer, Montserrat; a non-elected member of the legislative assembly, and a member of the executive council in that island; was appointed a justice of peace and provost-marshal 1853, which office he held until 1865, when he was promoted to the offices of colonial secretary and treasurer; acted as puisne justice in 1852; was a member of the executive legislative council in 1861; is registrar of deeds; registrar-general of births, &c.; comptroller of customs; and a member of the board of public works.
MEAGHER, JAMES.—Superintendent of public works, Trinidad; appointed Aug. 1863.

MELBOURNE (AUSTRALIA), 1st BISHOP OF. Founded 1847.—RIGHT REV. CHARLES PERRY, D.D.—Was formerly fellow of Trinity College, Cambridge, where he graduated senior wrangler, Smith's prizeman, and 1st class in classics, 1828; appointed 1st Bishop of Melbourne on the subdivision of the see of Australia, 1847.
MELIEUX, J.—Appointed clerk of the crown and recorder, Sierra Leone, 1847, and Sheriff, 1855.
MELLER, DR.—Director of botanical gardens, Mauritius, July, 1865.

MELLER, H. J.—Barrister of the Middle Temple, 1840; member of home circuit; appointed resident magistrate, D'Urban, Natal, 1850, to whose court increased civil jurisdiction was given, 1852, extending to £100, and trial by jury; was acting crown prosecutor from Oct. 1855 to Dec. 1856; acting puisne judge, supreme court, from July, 1861, for 12 months, and is colonial commandant of D'Urban rifle guard volunteers; acting 2nd puisne judge, 1864.
MELLISS, JOHN.—Surveyor and colonial engineer, St. Helena; appointed 1859; joined the colonial service in the engineer department of that island in 1851.
MENDIS, REV. A.—Appointed colonial chaplain, Morottoo and Corlewelle, Ceylon, Jan. 1861.
MERCATI, COUNT N.—Was appointed in 1850 secretary to the resident at Zante.

MERCER, W. T.—Graduated at Exeter College, Oxford, B.A., 1843; M.A. 1851; entered at the Inner Temple, 1842, but has not been called to the bar. Has been colonial secretary of Hong Kong since 1854; and a member of both councils. Entered the colonial service as private secretary to Sir J. Davis, governor of Hong Kong, 1844; appointed acting colonial treasurer, 1845; confirmed 1847; administered the government of Hong Kong from July to Sept. 1861, and again from July 1862 to Sept. 1864; again on the departure of Governor Sir H. Robinson for Ceylon, from March, 1865, to March, 1866.
MEREDITH, EDMUND A., L.L.D.—Assistant provincial secretary for Canada West.

MERIVALE, HERMAN, C.B. (Civil).—Was educated at Harrow, and at Oriel and Trinity Colleges, Oxford; Ireland scholar, 1825; graduated 1st class in classics, 1827; elected fellow of Balliol, 1828; obtained the chancellor's prize for English essay, 1830; was Eldon scholar, 1831, and professor of political economy, 1837; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Nov. 1832; practised on the western circuit; was recorder of Penzance, Falmouth, and Helston from 1841 to 1847; was appointed under-secretary of state for the colonies in 1848, and under-secretary of state for India, 1859; created a C.B., 1859; is author of "Lectures on Colonization and Colonies, delivered at Oxford."
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
MERRIMAN, VEN. N. J., M.A.—Colonial chaplain, Graham's Town, and archdeacon; appointed 1849.

MERSON, REV. C. (M.A. Aberdeen)—Appointed colonial chaplain, Scotch Church, Colombo, Ceylon, Oct. 1859; acted as colonial chaplain of the Dutch Presbyterian Church, Colombo, from Feb. 1860 to July 1861; and July 1861 to October 1865.
MESHAM, A.—Was clerk to the attorney-general, Natal, from 1856 to 1859; promoted registrar of the supreme court, and registrar of the Vice-Admiralty court in that colony, 1859.
MESSITER, EDWARD STEVENS.—Appointed police officer, Mauritius, Aug. 1847; police magistrate at Rodrigues, Nov. 1856; stipendiary magistrate at Grand Port, Mauritius, June, 1862.
MEURANT, L. H.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of Cradock division, Cape of Good Hope; appointed July, 1859; was magistrate's clerk at Stockenstrom, July, 1853 to 1859.
MICALLEF, SIR ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. and LL.D.—Was appointed crown advocate, Malta, Oct. 1842; one of Her Majesty's judges, Jan. 1854, and president of the court of appeal, July, 1859.
MILLAR, W.—Appointed engineer of the harbour steam tug, Natal, Nov. 1860.

MILLARD, FREDERICK B. A.—Educated at King's College school; entered Queen's College, Oxford, as Michel exhibitioner; graduated (B.A.) in 1860; senior classical master in the Edinburgh Institution to Sept. 1863, when appointed principal of grammar school, and inspector of schools, St. Christopher.

MILLER, J. T.—Employed in Colonial Office, 1835 to 1837; clerk to agent-general for emigration, Nov. 1837; clerk and registrar, colonial land and emigration commissioners, 1840 to 1847; appointed registrar, Colonial Office, Nov. 1847 to 1853; parliamentary clerk, Colonial Office, 1849 to 1856; assistant librarian, Colonial Office, 1858; transferred to Her Majesty's Treasury, Jan. 1859.

MILLS, CAPT.—Served in the 98th foot, and on the staff of Her Majesty's army in China, India, and Turkey, from Jan. 1843 to Dec. 1856; medal for Punjaub campaign and Medjidié; served as staff officer German military settlers at the Cape of Good Hope from Jan. 1857; and appointed sheriff of British Kaffraria, 1st Jan. 1862.
MISSO, M.B.—Appointed medical sub-assistant to governor of Ceylon, 1837; 3rd class, 1842; 2nd class, 1847; medical assistant, 1858.
MITCHELL, F. W.—Assistant postmaster-general, Hong Kong, 1860; postmaster-general, 1862.
MITCHELL, H.—Agent-general of immigrants, Trinidad; appointed 1853. Was assistant-superintendent from 1850 to 1853.
MITCHELL, SAMUEL.—Colonial secretary, registrar, and clerk of the executive council, Grenada; appointed 1857. Entered the colonial service, 1845; was chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1854.
MITCHELL, W. H.—Assistant magistrate and sheriff of Hong Kong, 1850; acting chief magistrate, 1852; retired on a pension, 1862.

MITFORD, EDWARD LEDWICH.—Extra assistant colonial secretary and clerk of the councils, Ceylon, from Feb. 1844, to March, 1845; writer, 30 Nov. 1844; assistant government agent, Kandy, Nov. 19, 1845; assistant government agent, Kailgallee, Sept. 1846; assistant government agent, Ratnapoora, Nov. 1847; district judge and assistant agent, 1849; acting government agent for the north-west province, Dec. 1856, to Aug. 1858, and

from June, 1859, to May, 1861; acting government agent for the eastern province, from Oct. 1862, to Nov. 1863; acting government agent for the north-western province, July, 1864; district judge of Jaffna and acting government agent, north-west province, Oct. 1865.

MOIR, ALEXANDER WILSON.—Entered the service in the customs' department in Jamaica in Feb. 1843; appointed presiding magistrate of the (late) colony of Bay Islands in Dec. 1855; public treasurer of Honduras, July, 1860; acting colonial secretary with a dormant commission as superintendent at the same place in June, 1861; president of the Turks and Caicos Islands in Aug. 1862.

MOIR, R. W. D.—Was appointed acting assistant government agent, commissioner of requests, and police magistrate, at Mulletivoe; Ceylon, 1856; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, 1861; assistant government agent at Mulletivoe, 1861; acting landing surveyor, Colombo, April, 1865; appointment confirmed, September, 1865; acting collector of customs, Galle, 1866.
MOLYNEUX, GEORGE.—Employed by Messrs. Watt & Co., engineers, Birmingham; appointed May 1865, to be blacksmith in the Hong Kong mint.

MONCK, 4th VISCT. (Ire. Creat. 1800); BARON MONCK, 1797.—CHARLES STANLEY MONCK.—Succeeded his father in 1849; educated at Trinity College, Dublin; called to the bar in Ireland, 1841; was a lord of the treasury from 1855 to 1858; is a deputy-lieut. of Wicklow; was an unsuccessful candidate at the Wicklow election, May, 1848; was M.P. for Portsmouth from 1852 to 1857; was appointed a commissioner of charitable donations and bequests (Ire.) 1851; and appointed captain-general and governor-in-chief of Canada, Oct. 1861.
MONTAGU, J. E.—Registrar of deeds, Cape of Good Hope, 1864; entered the service in the colonial office, 1844; was appointed chief clerk, 1852; under colonial secretary, 1858.

MONTANARO, GIUSEPPE.—Served in the commissariat department at Malta from 1806 to 1829; was purveyor of charitable institutions and collector of land revenue, and appointed cashier of the treasury, Malta, 1859. Is ex-officio member of the council of government.

MONTEAGLE, 1st BARON (United Kingdom, Creat. 1839); Privy Councillor, 1834.—THOMAS SPRING RICE, F.R.S.—Was educated at the University of Cambridge; studied for the bar for some time, but relinquished the profession on his first marriage; was under-secretary of state for the home department in 1827; secretary to the treasury from Nov. 1830 till June, 1834; secretary for the colonies from July to Nov. 1834; chancellor of the exchequer from April, 1835, to Sept. 1839, when he succeeded Sir John Newport as comptroller-general of the exchequer. Represented Limerick in parliament from 1820 to 1832, and the borough of Cambridge from 1832 to 1839.

MONTGOMERY, ALEXANDER.—Was appointed a clerk in the colonial secretary's office at Mauritius, March, 1848; clerk in the internal revenue office, Jan. 1854; and first clerk in the council office in that colony, Jan. 1859.

MONTREAL, 2nd BISHOP OF. Founded 1836. —RIGHT REV. FRANCIS FULFORD, D.D.—Educated at Tiverton grammar school, and at Exeter College, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1824, and was elected a fellow, June, 1825; received the degree of D.D. in 1850; was rector of Trowbridge, Wilts, from 1832 to 1842; rector of Croydon, Cambridgeshire, from 1842 to 1845; was
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.

minister of Curzon Chapel, in the parish of St. George's, Hanover Square, London, from 1845 till his consecration in 1850; was also chaplain to the Duchess of Gloucester. This diocese is situated in Lower Canada, and was formerly a portion of the diocese of Quebec. Annual value of the see £800, from the colonial bishoprics' fund, &c.
MONTY, P. P.—Appointed first clerk in the civil status office at Mauritius, Feb. 1857.

MOODIE, W. J. D.—Was employed in the office of the colonial secretary of the Cape of Good Hope, 1843; in that of the colonial secretary of Natal from 1846 to 1859, in which he became chief clerk; was appointed resident magistrate, Lower Umcomas, 1860.

MOODY, RICHARD CLEMENT.—Entered the royal engineers, Nov. 1830; promoted to be captain, March, 1844; lieut.-colonel, Jan. 1855; and colonel, April, 1858; appointed lieut.-governor of the Falkland Islands, May, 1841; governor, June, 1843; which appointment he held until 1847; appointed chief commissioner of lands and works in British Columbia, Sept. 1858, which appointment he held until Dec. 1863; now commands at Chatham.

MOORE, THOS. E. L.—Is a captain in the royal navy on half-pay list; was mate of the 'Terror' in Ross's antarctic expedition, 1843; and commander of the 'Plover' engaged in the Behring Straits arctic searching expedition, 1848-51; was appointed governor of the Falkland Islands, July, 1855.

MOOYAART, REV. E., M.A.—Employed as acting colonial chaplain at Kandy, Galle, and Trincomalee, Ceylon, from 1844 to 1851; appointed colonial chaplain, Kandyan districts, May, 1852; colonial chaplain, Newera Ellia, 1853; colonial chaplain, Galle, 1858; colonial chaplain of Kandy and archdeacon of Colombo, Aug. 1864.

MOOYAART, H.—Appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, 1848; commissioner of requests at Malagam, 1849; assistant government agent, Newera Ellia, 1853; assistant government agent, Kaigalle, 1853; acting assistant government agent, Kurnegalle, 1854; acting assistant agent and district judge, Ratnapoora, 1856; resumed duties at Kaigalle, 1858; assistant government agent at Badulla, 1862; retired on pension, 1865.
MORALES, C. M.—Speaker of the house of assembly, Jamaica; appointed 1849.
MORELAND, J. H. B.—Appointed clerk in the audit office, Natal, 1852; and first clerk in that office, May, 1859; promoted to be landing surveyor in the customs department, March, 1864.

MORGAN, JOHN.—Assistant harbour-master, Port Louis, Mauritius; appointed March, 1860. Entered the colonial service in October, 1852, as mate of the colonial steamer; was promoted to the office of pilot in March, 1854.
MORGAN, J. T., M.R.C.S., England.—Appointed medical assistant to government of Ceylon, 1861.

MORGAN, R. F.—Admitted an advocate of the supreme court of Ceylon, 1846; district judge of Colombo, Ceylon, Oct. 1856; acting puisne judge of the supreme court in that colony, 1857; called to the English bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov. 1858; acting Queen's advocate, 1857; acting senior puisne judge, 1860; district judge of Colombo, 1860; acting Queen's advocate, April, 1861; resumed duties as district judge, Colombo, Nov. 1861; acting deputy Queen's advocate for the island; appointed Queen's advocate Jan. 1, 1863; and allowed private practice; is one of the law officers of the crown in the colony.
MORGAN, O. W. C.—Was appointed acting

deputy Queen's advocate for the northern circuit, Ceylon, Jan. 1857; acting deputy Queen's advocate, Kurnegalle, Oct. 15, 1858; deputy Queen's advocate, southern circuit, 1863; acted as deputy Queen's advocate, Midland circuit from January to November, 1863.

MORPHEW, J.—Acting civil engineer to the government of Ceylon, 1844; acting assistant government agent at Kandy, 1846; assistant government agent, commissioner of requests, &c. Hambantotte, 1849; assistant government agent, &c. Batticaloa, 1854; acting district judge, Trincomalie, 1860; resumed duties at Batticaloa, 1862; district judge Jaffna, 1865.
MORRIS, JAMES.—Worked for twelve years as roller in Mr. Cooper's foundry, at Birmingham; appointed Feb. 1865 foreman of the rolling mill in the mint of Hong Kong.

MORRIS, R. W. T.—Appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, 1853; acting assistant government agent, Kandy, 1853; assistant government agent, Jaffna, 1855; commissioner of requests, Dambool, 1858; acting district judge at Badulla, 1859; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Jaffna, 1860; acting assistant government agent, &c. Nuwerakalawiya, 1861; assistant government agent, &c. Kaigalle, Oct. 1862; to continue to act at Newera Kalawiya; assistant government agent, Batticaloa, 1865.

MORRIS, W.—Was formerly in the army; ensign, 97th Reg. April, 1825; lieut. 1832; appointed assistant commissary general at Trincomalie, Ceylon, 1835; assistant government agent, and district judge, Hambantotte, 1836; assistant agent and district judge, Newera Ellia, 1837; assistant agent, Galle, 1837; assistant agent, Kurnegalle, 1839; government agent for north-western province, 1853; government agent for eastern province 1855; acting government agent, &c. for the south, ern province, Sept. 1862; acting government agent &c., for southern province, June, 1865; confirmed October, 1865.

MORRISON, WM. LAWTIE.—Entered the royal engineers, 1843; promoted to be 1st lieut. April, 1846; captain, Feb. 1854; was appointed superintendent of public works at Barbados, 1857; and surveyor-general of Mauritius, July, 1861. Is on seconded list, and does not receive any regimental pay.

MOSELEY, HENRY EDWARD.—Was Second clerk in the treasurer's department, Bahamas, from Aug. 8, 1863, to Aug. 31st, 1865; appointed librarian to the Nassau public library and clerk to the board of public works at the same place, on July, 1865.

MUNDY, ROBERT MILLER.—Cadet Royal military academy, Woolwich, Feb. 1828; lieutenant Royal artillery, June, 1833; lieutenant Royal horse artillery, March 1841 to April 1844; 2nd captain, 1844; brevet major, Oct. 1846; invalided on half-pay, Oct. 1847; appointed magistrate for county of Hants, June, 1851; served as lieutenant-colonel, Osmanli horse artillery in Turkey, March 1855 to Aug. 1856; is lieut.-colonel in the Turkish service, and has received 3rd class medjidie; appointed lieutenant-governor of Granada, Sept. 1863; administering government of Barbados, 10th April, 1865.

MUNNIK, J. A.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of the Tulbagh division of Cape Colony; appointed 1856; was clerk to the civil commissioner at Worcester, 1832 to 1853; clerk of the peace, Clanwilliam, April 1853 to 1856.
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.

MUNRO, G. G.—Appointed acting landing-waiter and searcher of customs for Carriacon, 22nd March, 1834; landing-waiter and searcher, Port of St. George, Grenada, Aug., 1834; surveying officer, 6th June, 1839; landing and tide surveyor, 24th July, 1840; Collector of customs, Tortola, 15th Sept., 1840, retained office until its abolition in 1852; appointed police magistrate, eastern district, Grenada, 5th Feb., 1861; immigration agent, 23rd May, 1863.

MURDOCH, T. W. C.—Appointed an extra clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, Aug. 1826; placed on the establishment of that office, July, 1828; was chief secretary in Canada from Sept. 1839 to Aug. 1842; précis writer in the colonial office, 1846, and chairman of the colonial land and emigration commissioners, Nov. 1847.
MURRAY, C. A.—Writer, Ceylon civil service, 1866; acting police magistrate, Dumbara, in the same year.
MURRAY, E.—Marshal of the courts, Trinidad; appointed 1851; was registrar of slaves.

MURRAY, MAJ.-GEN. FREEMAN.—Served with 64th regiment from June, 1825, to Sept. 1827 at Gibraltar; from 1827 to 1834 at home; from 1834 to 1836 with 60th royal rifles at Malta; from 1836 to 1837 at Gibraltar; from 1837 to 1839 in the Ionian Islands, where he served as brigade-major; appointed aide-de-camp to the lord-lieutenant of Ireland in 1839; served on that staff until 1843, when he became assistant military secretary to the commander of the forces in Ireland; resigned that appointment to accompany the 60th rifles to India; served in India and Scinde from 1845 to 1847; became lieut.-colonel in 17th regiment in 1847; appointed to the command of the 72nd Highlanders in 1849; joined that regiment at Barbados, West Indies, and proceeded to Trinidad, where he commanded the troops until his regiment was ordered to North America in 1850; commanded the troops in New Brunswick from that time to 1853; administered the government of the colony of New Brunswick from May to Sept. 1852, during the absence in England of the lieut.-governor; was quartered in Halifax, Nova Scotia, from 1853 to 1854, when he was appointed governor of Bermuda, which government he held until the 14th of March, 1861.

MURRAY, JOHN IVOR.—M.D. and L.R.C.S., Edinburgh, 1845; F.R.C.S., Edinburgh, 1856; F.R.S.E., 1857; acting staff surgeon with the British army in the Crimea in 1855 and 1856, for which he received the Crimean and Turkish medals; appointed colonial surgeon, Hong Kong, 1858. Is also surgeon to the Lock hospital, in that colony.

MUSGRAVE, ANTHONY.—Was private secretary to Mr. Mackintosh when governor-in-chief of the Leeward Islands, 1850-51; entered as student at the Inner Temple, 1851; appointed treasury accountant at Antigua, 1852; resumed legal studies at the Temple in 1853; appointed colonial secretary of Antigua, Feb. 1854; administrator of the colony of Nevis, Oct. 1860; administrator of government of St. Vincent, April, 1861; appointed lieut.-governor of St. Vincent, May, 1862; governor of Newfoundland, April, 1864.

MUSSON, E. H.—Appointed immigration agent and acting clerk of executive council and administrative committee, St. Vincent, 1859; confirmed in first office, 1860, and in the latter, 1861; ex-officio auditor of public accounts, 1863; appointed auditor of public accounts under per-
manent act establishing the office, 1864; is captain of militia, and garrison adjutant.

MUSSON, SAMUEL PAYNTER.—Appointed a clerk in the governor's private secretary's office, Jamaica, 1st April, 1866. On change of constitution, appointed a second-class clerk in the office of the financial secretary, Jamaica, 16th September, 1866.
MUTUKISNA, H. F.—Was appointed acting deputy Queen's advocate, Jaffna, Ceylon, 1859; in which appointment he was confirmed, July, 1862.
MYERS, WILLIAM ROBERTSON.—Secretary of executive committee, and clerk of privy council, Jamaica; appointed 1854.

NAIRN, WILLIAM E., B.A.—Entered the government service in Tasmania, 1838, in the colonial secretary's office; police magistrate, 1840; comptroller-general and sheriff, 1856; president of the legislative council in Tasmania, 1859.
NARRAWAY. — Employed in the colonial office as an extra clerk, since 1831.

NASSAU.—Patent, Nov., 1861; 1st bishop, Dr. C. Caulfield. 2nd bishop, Dr. A. R. P. Venables, graduate of Exeter College, Oxford, 1848; late curate of St. Paul's Church, Oxford; appointed 1863. The diocese comprises the Bahama Islands, Turks, and Caicos Islands, the population being about 38,700. It contains 13 parishes and 2 districts.

NATAL, 1st BISHOP OF. Founded 1853.—RIGHT REV. JOHN WILLIAM COLENSO, D.D.—Educated at the Devonport proprietary school and at St. John's College, Cambridge, where he obtained a fellowship; graduated B.A. 1836 (when he was 2nd wrangler and Smith's prizeman); M.A., 1839; D.D., 1853; was fellow of St. John's College from 1837 to 1846; mathematical master at Harrow school from 1838 to 1842; rector of Fornsett St. Mary, Norfolk, from 1846 to 1853; consecrated 1st Bishop of this see, 1853. Is author of "Elements of Algebra," and other educational works. This diocese, originally part of the see of Cape Town, comprises the district of Natal.
NAUDI, SALVATORE, LL.D.—Appointed a judge of the court of appeal. Malta, July, 1859.
NEEDHAM, JOSEPH.—Chief justice of Vancouver Island, 1865; is a member of the English bar.
NELL, GEO. FRED., B.C.L., Cambridge.—Barrister-at-law; acting deputy Queen's advocate, at Galle, in 1861; and appointed deputy Queen's advocate for north-western province, 1863.

NEIL, LOUIS.—Advocate of the supreme court of Ceylon; appointed acting deputy Queen's advocate for the southern circuit at Galle, in January, 1863; then appointed to the northern circuit in April, 1863; acting again at Galle from November, 1865.

NELSON (NEW ZEALAND), 1st BISHOP OF.—RIGHT REV. EDMUND HOBHOUSE.—Educated at Eton and at Balliol College, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. 1838; M.A. (Merton) 1842; B.D. 1850; D.D. 1858; elected fellow of Merton, 1841; appointed vicar of St. Peter in the East, Oxford, 1843, and consecrated 1st Bishop of this see, 1858.
NELSON, 2nd BISHOP OF.—RIGHT REV. ANDREW BURN SUTEN.—Of Trinity College, Cambridge; lately incumbent of All Saints, Mile End New Town; consecrated 1866.

NESBITT, CHARLES R.—Was police magistrate at Nassau, 1831; appointed colonial secretary, registrar of records, and clerk of the council, Bahamas, 1838. Held dormant commissions as lieut. governor, 1841 and 1844; administered the government of St. Vincent from Jan. 1860 to June, 1861
Digitized by Google
COLONIAL OFFICE LIST.
and appointed administrator of the government of Bahamas, June, 1861, and again in 1864.

NESBITT, EDWIN J.—Was in the colonial secretary's office, Bahamas, during 1860 and 1861; acted as secretary to the board of education of that colony, Feb. 1862; appointed acting clerk of the house of assembly, Vancouver's Island, Nov. 1862; chief clerk in office of colonial secretary, Jan. 1863.

NEWCASTLE (AUSTRALIA), 2nd BISHOP of. Founded 1847.—RIGHT REV. WILLIAM TYRRELL, D.D.—Educated at Charterhouse school, and at St. John's College, Cambridge, where he obtained a scholarship and was 4th senior optime; graduated B.A. 1831, and M.A. 1834; ordained 1832; appointed 1st Bishop of Newcastle in 1847, on the division of the bishopric of Australia.

NEWFOUNDLAND, 2nd Bishop of. Founded 1839.—RIGHT REV. EDWARD FEILD, D.D.—Educated at Rugby, where he obtained the prize for the best Latin poem in 1820; and at Queen's College, Oxford, where he obtained a Michel fellowship; was first class in mathematics, 1823, and second class in classics, and graduated M.A., 1826; appointed public examiner in 1827; was presented by his college to the living of English-Bicknor, Gloucestershire, in 1833, and consecrated Bishop of Newfoundland in 1834. The diocese includes Newfoundland and the Bermudas, and contains a population of 124,300 souls.

NEWLAND, CHAS. FRANKLAND.—Commander, R.N.—Appointed comptroller of convicts at Bermuda, March, 1859; appointed comptroller-general of convicts in Western Australia, 1862; is a member of the executive council.
NEWTON, EDWARD.—Assistant colonial secretary of Mauritius; appointed May, 1859; acting auditor-general, September, 1863; auditor-general, April, 1866.
NEWTON, WILLIAM.—Was employed in the surveyor-general's department at Mauritius, Feb. 1832; promoted to be inspector of works, Feb. 1846.
NEW WESTMINSTER (BRITISH COLUMBIA), 1st BISHOP of, 1865.—REV. JOHN POSTLETHWAITE, graduated 1851, Trinity College, Cambridge; lately incumbent of Coatham, near Redcar.

NEW ZEALAND, 1st Bishop of. Founded 1841.—RIGHT REV. GEORGE AUGUSTUS SELWYN, D.D.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; was a junior optime in mathematics, and 1st class in classics in 1831; consecrated 1st Bishop of New Zealand in 1841; was curate of Windsor previous to his consecration. The diocese was reconstituted in 1858 and made metropolitan over the sees of Wellington, Waiapu, Nelson, and Christchurch.

NICHOLSON, 1st BART. (Creat. 1859), KNT. BACHEL. 1852.—SIR CHAS. NICHOLSON, D.C.L.—Emigrated to Australia in 1834, and practised there as a physician; was elected a member of the first legislative council of New South Wales in 1843, and was three times chosen for the office of speaker (1845 to 1856); is provost of the University of Sydney.
NICHOLSON, JOHN WM.—Appointed landing-waiter in the customs' department at the Mauritius, August, 1845, and landing-surveyor, October, 1856.
